metadata.source: Red Square Exam Intensive MCS P.5 Mathematics Term 3 Examination, Revision Papers 2, 3, 4, 5
metadata.notes: Questions requiring diagrams have been omitted. Fractions represented as unicode or text (e.g. 3/4, 1 2/5).

questions.text: In the product of 79.8 × 0.04, what is the place value of the digit '2'?
Round off '3,069,784' to the nearest ten thousand.
What is the difference between the values of the digit '5' in '235,003' and '134,005'?

In 2009, the arrivals of passengers by air and by land in Hong Kong were 14,936,000 and 85,251,000 respectively. What was the difference in number between these two modes of transport? (Round off the number to the nearest million.)
If 243,678 > Y > 241,987, which is a possible value of Y?
What is the sum of the smallest 6-digit odd number and the largest 4-digit even number?
Which of the following has its value nearest to 4.5?
Round off the number '36,189,824' to the nearest ten thousand.
Mr. Chan has $456,217 in his bank account. Rewrite the amount of money he has to the nearest thousand dollars.
What is the difference between the values of the digits '4' and '7' in the number '8,450,670'?
Which of the following fractions is a possible answer for □ where 3/4 > □ > 5/7?
Arrange the following fractions in ascending order: 1 6/13, 1 17/39, 1 1/3, 2 1/13
A 2 1/2 L bottle of grape juice is sold for $16. Ben drank 1/4 of a bottle. How much grape juice did he drink?
Calculate 4 2/7 ÷ 6
Katelyn has 4 5/8 L of paint. She pours them into small pots of 5/6 L. How much paint is left?
There was 2 3/4 L of orange juice in a jar. Mary drank 7/8 L, Justin drank 7/16 L, and Fred drank 10/11 L. Who drank the most?
When calculating 1.42 × 0.9, Bella calculated 142 × 9 instead. Her answer is how many times that of the correct answer?
Which of the following expressions is most suitable for estimating the value of 1.487 − 2.629 + 6.371?
Mrs. Cheung baked a pound cake with 0.375 kg of flour and 0.125 kg of butter. How much flour was needed to bake 6 such cakes?
Which of the following has the smallest value?
Which of the following expressions has a different answer?
The capacity of Bottle A is 1.5 L and 0.42 L of water is needed to fill it up. Bottle B contains 1.3 L of water. What is the difference in the amount of water contained in Bottle A and Bottle B?
The side length of a square scarf is 0.68 m. What is the area of the scarf? (Give the answer correct to the nearest hundredth.)
Which of the following sets of numbers has 15 as their H.C.F.?
List all the prime numbers from 50 to 70 inclusive.
8 is a common multiple of ___.
What is the sum of the prime numbers from 30 to 50?
List out all the prime numbers from 70 to 90.
List all the prime numbers from 40 to 55 inclusive.
Which of the following numbers is a prime number?
How many common factors do 18 and 81 have?
Jack writes three numbers on a card: 15, 18 and 20. Which of the following statements is correct?
options: A. 5 is a common factor of the three numbers
B. The L.C.M. of the three numbers is 30
C. The H.C.F. of the three numbers is 1
D. 2 is a common multiple of 18 and 20

questions.text: Which of the following stands for 'divide y by 8 and then add 3'?
Jenny has 64 stickers. Jenny has 8 more stickers than Brenda. If Brenda has j stickers, which of the following equations is correct?
There are 150 pupils in Primary 5. M of them are boys. Half of the girls are wearing orange summer uniform. How many girls are wearing orange summer uniform?
Ann had 10 boxes of marbles. In each box there were Y yellow marbles and 8 red marbles. Altogether she had 120 marbles. Which equation finds the number of yellow marbles?
Solve the equation: 18t = 6
The base and height of a triangle are r cm and 13 cm respectively. Its area is 104 cm². Set an equation to find the base of the triangle.
The cost of a bottle of liquid soap was $30. Mrs Ho bought 9 bottles of liquid soap. She had $210 left. How much money did Mrs Ho have at first? (Use equation to solve the problem.)
Henry has Y rulers that cost $5 each and 10 rulers that cost $7 each. Write an algebraic expression to show the total value of the rulers.
The base and height of a triangle are P cm and 6 cm respectively. Write an algebraic expression to show the area of the triangle.
Mrs. Chan bought a dress in an online shop. The shipping fee was $30. The total cost of the dress and shipping fee was $345. What was the cost of the dress? (Use equation to solve the problem.)
Mr. Lee had 100 carrots. After selling some carrots in the morning, there were 46 carrots left in the afternoon. How many carrots did Mr. Lee sell in the morning? (Use equation.)
Durian was sold at $39.50 per kilogram. Find the cost of 2.6 kg of durian.
The cost of a dozen pens is $y, which is 3 times the cost of a dozen pencils. What is the cost of a pencil?
Mary weighs 56 2/5 kg. She is 2 3/10 kg lighter than Tony. What is Tony's weight?
Tina weighs 45.8 kg. The weight of her father is 1.5 times that of Tina. What is her father's weight?
Mrs. Lee makes a dress with 2 3/4 m of cloth. If one metre of cloth costs $46, how much does she need to pay for making 2 such dresses?
Ann has 0.5 times as many marbles as May. If May has 152 marbles, how many marbles does Ann have?
A T-shirt costs $y. A dress costs 3 times as much as a T-shirt. How much should be paid for 2 dresses?
Mrs. Wong bought a TV set. She paid in instalments of $M a month for 18 months. Find the price of the TV set if M = 800.
Dick runs from a lamppost to another lamppost and back again three times each morning. He runs 532 4/5 m in total. What is the distance between the two lampposts?
A machine can produce a toy boat in 2 2/5 minutes. How many toy boats can be made in 1 hour?
Sam can carpet 1 m² of the floor in 1/3 hour. If he works 4 hours a day, find the area of the floor he can carpet in a week (5 working days).
70 2/5 kg of flour is packed into boxes. Each box carries 5 1/5 kg. How much flour is left?
questions.answer: 0 kg (exact — 70 2/5 ÷ 5 1/5 = 13 and 1/2 remainder check: 13 × 5 1/5 = 67 6/5... answer: 3/5 kg)

questions.text: The price of 4 2/5 m² of tiles is $27 1/2. Denise buys 7 m² of tiles. How much should she pay?
Ronald pays two $20 notes for a toy bear priced at $24.80. How much change should he get?
Each kilogram of organic potatoes costs $52.90. Sally buys 1,070 g of potatoes. How much should she pay? (Correct the answer to the nearest tenth.)
There are 275 g of clay in a bag. Tiffany takes away 2/5 of the clay to make 4 models. On average, how many grams of clay does she use to make one model?
Sandy collected 8 1/2 kg of vegetables today. Yesterday, she collected 2 1/5 kg more than today. How many kilograms of vegetables did she collect in these two days?
Mrs. Chan weighs 52 4/5 kg. The weight of Mr. Chan is 1 4/11 times the weight of Mrs. Chan. The weight of Billy is 2/5 of the weight of Mr. Chan. What is the weight of Mr. Chan?
A bottle 3/5 full contains 1 1/2 L of water. A jug uses 7 1/2 L of water. How many such bottles of water are needed to fill the jug?
3/4 dozen pieces of cake weigh 355 1/2 g. Find the weight of each piece of cake.
A piece of ribbon 12 1/2 m long is cut into 5 equal parts. Find the length of each part.
Sally has 12 1/2 kg of chicken wings. She puts 2/5 of the chicken wings into bags of 5/8 kg. How many bags does she need?
A bottle can hold 2 1/5 L of water. A jar can hold 1 7/8 L. A pot can hold 4 11/20 L. How much more water can the jar and the pot hold in total than the bottle?
A 3/10 L bottle of olive oil costs $8 2/5. How many such bottles can be bought with $70?
Jim needs 1/5 h to make a model. At most how many models can he make in 1 1/4 h?
A pen is 14 3/10 cm long and a rubber is 5 4/5 cm long. The length of a pencil is 2 1/4 cm shorter than the total length of the pen and the rubber. What is the length of the pencil?
Mr. Chan had 7 11/21 m of cloth. He used 4 2/7 m to make a shirt. Then he bought another 2 1/6 m of cloth. How much cloth did he have at last?
One kilogram of grapes costs $45.20. Mr. Wong bought a bunch of grapes which weighed 0.78 kg. How much should he pay (to the nearest dollar)?
A cloth 8 4/5 m long can be exactly cut into 4 equal pieces. What is the length of each piece?
There are 196 poultries in a farm. 5/14 of the poultries are chickens and 3/14 of them are ducks. Find the total number of chickens and ducks.
Mary weighs 36 2/5 kg. Amy's weight is 1 1/4 times the weight of Mary's. What is Amy's weight?
Stephaine runs around a square court 8 times. If she runs 12 4/5 km in total, what is the side length of the square court?
Mrs Wilson bought 13 1/8 kg of potatoes which was 4/5 kg more than the amount bought by Mrs Jones. How many kilograms of potatoes did they buy altogether?
3 1/2 m of cloth is used to make a dress. How many dresses can be made with a piece of cloth 25 1/5 m long?
A box of milk costs $4.60. A box of orange juice costs $5.10. What will be the change if Ellsy pays $100 for 4 boxes of milk and 5 boxes of orange juice?
Susan had 216 balloons. 1/8 of the balloons were pink and 7/12 of the balloons were red. The remaining balloons were yellow. How many yellow balloons did Susan have?
A container holds 22 1/2 L of milk when it is 5/8 full. What is the volume of milk in the container when it is 3/4 full?
A rectangle is made up of 2 identical squares. If the area of each square is 36 cm², find the perimeter of the rectangle.
The side of an equilateral triangle is 7.24 cm. What is its perimeter?
The height of a trapezium is 18 cm and its area is 198 cm². If the perimeter of the trapezium is 52 cm, what is the sum of the two non-parallel sides?
A parallelogram is made up of 2 identical triangles. Each triangle has a base of 14 cm and a height of 8 cm. Find the area of the parallelogram.
The length of a rectangle is 7 1/2 cm. The width is 8/15 of its length. What is the area of the rectangle?
The base of a parallelogram is 36.6 cm. Its height is 0.75 times of its base. Find its area. (Correct the answer to the nearest tenth.)
The area of a parallelogram is 108 m². Find the base of the parallelogram if the height is 12 m.
How many 7-cm squares can be cut from a piece of paper which is 29 cm long and 23 cm wide at most?
The side length of a square is 4.6 cm. What is the area of the square?
The upper base and lower base of a trapezium are 18 cm and 24 cm respectively. The area is 168 cm². Find the height of the trapezium.
The perimeter of an isosceles triangle is 39 cm. Two equal sides are each 11 cm. The base is 9 cm. Find the area of the triangle.
The volume of a cuboid is 135 cm³. The length is 8 cm and the width is 3 3/4 cm. Find its height.
Which of the following 3-D shapes does not have a triangular face?
Name the 3-D shape which has only 1 face.
The length, width and height of a cuboid are 16 cm, 7 cm and 4 cm respectively. It is cut into 8 identical cuboids. What is the volume of each small cuboid?
Which 3-D shape has a circular base and a curved lateral face?
What is the 3-D shape which has 6 identical square faces?
Alice has a storage box in the shape of a cuboid. It is 1 1/2 m long, 3/5 m wide, and its volume is 1 2/25 m³. Find its height.
Name TWO types of 3-D shapes which have 8 vertices or more.
A rectangular box is 27 cm long, 9 cm wide and 6 cm high. How many 3-cm cubes can be put in the rectangular box at most?
Denise cuts out the largest cube from an 18 cm long, 8 cm wide and 4 cm tall cuboid. Find the volume of the remaining part of the cuboid.
Which of the following 3-D shapes has a different number of edges?
A 3-D shape has 8 faces, 12 vertices and 18 edges. Name the 3-D shape.
The volume of a cuboid is 135 cm³. Its length is 8 cm and its width is 3 3/4 cm. Find its height.
Sally wants to make a rectangular pyramid with plasticines and toothpicks. She has got 4 toothpicks. How many more toothpicks and plasticines does she need?
A solid is made up of a cuboid and a cube. The volume of the solid and the cube are 108 cm³ and 27 cm³ respectively. The cuboid has a square cross-section of side 3 cm. Find the length of the cuboid.
PQ is the radius of Circle A and RQ is the radius of Circle B. If RQ is y cm long, find the diameter of Circle B in terms of y.
P is the centre of the circle. The perimeter of triangle ABC is 8 cm. One side AB = 3 cm and BC = 1.6 cm. Find the length of the radius of the circle.
In the diagram, O is the centre of the circle. Which of the following lines is twice as long as OR?
Which of the following statements about circles is incorrect?
options: A. The lengths of the diameters of a circle are the same
B. There is only 1 centre in a circle
C. The length around a circle is the circumference of the circle
D. The radius is the longest line in the circle

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: A. 2 identical trapeziums can form a rhombus
B. A trapezium has 2 pairs of parallel opposite sides
C. A rhombus has 2 pairs of equal opposite sides
D. All quadrilaterals have 4 right angles

questions.text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: A. A rectangle has four right angles
B. An isosceles triangle has two sides equal in length
C. A rhombus has only one pair of parallel sides
D. A parallelogram has two pairs of parallel sides

questions.text: A kite with sides 2.9 cm and 4.2 cm is folded into 2 triangles. Which of the following statements is correct?
options: C. An equilateral triangle and an isosceles triangle are formed
D. An isosceles triangle and a right-angled triangle are formed

questions.text: Which of the following is correct?
Paul ran 3 1/5 laps on Monday, 3 4/25 laps on Tuesday and 2 3/8 laps on Wednesday. Which of the following statements is correct?
options: A. Paul ran the most number of laps on Tuesday
B. Paul ran less than 8 laps on these three days in total
C. Paul ran 6 9/25 laps on Monday and Tuesday altogether
D. Paul ran more than 2 1/2 laps on Wednesday

questions.text: On a map, the Market is to the southeast of the Library. The Bank is directly south of the Park and directly east of the Market. The Bank is to the ___ of the Market.
Peter goes from the Bank to the Park. Each grid square on the map is 300 m × 300 m. He walks directly northwest. How far does he walk?
